name: Smiley Cactus
description: A cheerful potted saguaro with two upraised arms, a pink flower bud on its crown, and two little black bead eyes — sitting in a flared terracotta pot with rim and soil.
feedback: Original whimsy subagent attempt timed out: a fairy mushroom house using hull-of-disks for the stem, difference() for the doorway, and intersection-with-slab for the cap — three CGAL-heavy ops + 7 labels — exceeded a 5-minute per-task ceiling. Replaced with an all-additive cactus that compiles in seconds. The label-aware path is fine; the underlying CGAL backend slows nonlinearly when many top-level statements use heavy CSG, which agents should be warned about up front: 'lazy-union splits, but each statement still runs through CGAL — keep individual statements light.'

Per-statement compile cost isn't surfaced anywhere. A timing breakdown per labelled region in `runScadAsync`'s return value would let agents see WHICH statement is slow and fix that one, rather than blanket-reducing $fn across the whole model.

The 'lazy-union splits top-level statements' optimization works, but the AMF round-trip seems to inflate triangle counts at high $fn (a single $fn=32 sphere went from ~1024 expected tris to ~9k in the labelled path's mesh output). Worth a closer look — possibly the merge-vector welding isn't happening on per-object meshes before compose.
